title: End of Evangelion Yasuyuki Satos 1998 translation
display_title: End of Evangelion Yasuyuki Satos 1998 translation
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion: Yasuyuki Sato's 1998 translation             Quote Post by gwern » Sun May 15, 2011 7:23 pm Doing my usual EML archive read, I noticed an offhand reference to an independent EoE translation by then-UCLA student Yasuyuki Sato. I failed to find any copies online, so I emailed him, and amazingly it worked. He graciously sent me an old email copy, and told me 'Do whatever you want with it. I don't care.' It can be found at: http://dl.dropbox.com/u/5317066/eva/scripts/1998-yasuyukisato-eoe.txt I have not compared it line for line with Bochan_bird or the A2000A translations*; the only interesting difference I've noticed so far is the 'Komm Susser Tod' scene doesn't attribute Asuka's final 'no' to her, so it seems to be true that in some versions that line was not spoken but teloped. This may be of interest to those looking at individual EoE lines, in the vein of http://www.gwern.net/komm-susser-tod#lyrics comparing 3 translations. * Bochan_bird's are available at evaotaku.com of course; I'm not sure where I got the A2000A translations from, but you can see them at http://dl.dropbox.com/u/5317066/eva/scripts/eoe-2000a-ep25.txt and http://dl.dropbox.com/u/5317066/eva/scripts/eoe-2000a-ep26.txt

[]: Re: End of Evangelion: Yasuyuki Sato's 1998 translation             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Sun May 15, 2011 7:35 pm Yasuyuki Sato wrote: Shinji___I'm so fucked up...

[]: Re: End of Evangelion: Yasuyuki Sato's 1998 translation             Quote Post by gwern » Sun May 15, 2011 7:49 pm Tarnsman wrote: Did he work for Mangle?

[]: Re: End of Evangelion: Yasuyuki Sato's 1998 translation             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun May 15, 2011 9:41 pm Tarnsman wrote: Did he work for Mangle?

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed May 25, 2011 5:16 pm Thanks for posting the translation! I found that this is more accurate than Bochan_bird or the A2000A translation.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Fri Aug 26, 2011 9:40 pm I've also found the earlier 1997 Kiyoshi Kawakami translation: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=489071#489071

I failed to find any copies online, so I emailed him, and amazingly it worked. He graciously sent me an old email copy, and told me 'Do whatever you want with it. I don't care.' It can be found at: http://dl.dropbox.com/u/5317066/eva/scripts/1998-yasuyukisato-eoe.txt I have not compared it line for line with Bochan_bird or the A2000A translations*; the only interesting difference I've noticed so far is the 'Komm Susser Tod' scene doesn't attribute Asuka's final 'no' to her, so it seems to be true that in some versions that line was not spoken but teloped.
